title: thymus fallax var. pseudomarschallianus
intro: Thymus fallax var. pseudomarschallianus, a lesser-known variety of the thyme plant, is a type of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) known for its small, pink or white flowers and its aromatic leaves. The leaves of this plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Thymus fallax var. pseudomarschallianus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be added to topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
